description:
Set up an automated CI/CD path for deploying the static Next.js UI artifact to the S3/CloudFront hosting target without expanding the broader production deployment scope owned by T-037.

**Scope**
- Add a GitHub Actions workflow or equivalent documented CI job that builds the app with `npm run build:static`.
- Discover `StaticAssetsBucketName` and `StaticWebDistributionDomainName`/distribution ID from the staging CloudFormation stack outputs instead of hard-coding generated values.
- Sync `out/` to the staging S3 static assets bucket with deletion of removed assets.
- Create a CloudFront invalidation for changed static routes/assets after upload.
- Use GitHub OIDC/AWS role assumption; do not introduce long-lived AWS access keys or store secrets in GitHub variables.
- Keep API/Lambda/CDK infrastructure deployment separate from this UI asset publish workflow unless explicitly invoked.
- Document manual fallback commands for local/operator-driven staging publishes.

**Acceptance**
- A clean run publishes the current static UI to the staging S3 bucket and invalidates CloudFront.
- The workflow can be run manually against staging and is safe to wire to `main` once owner trust restrictions from T-084 are confirmed.
- The deployed CloudFront URL serves `/`, `/intake`, and `/onboarding/mdi` with API paths still routed to API Gateway.
- Failed builds or failed sync/invalidation stop the workflow and do not partially claim success.
- No PHI, vendor secrets, Cognito secrets, or AWS long-lived credentials are stored in repo or GitHub secrets.
title: CI/CD workflow: publish static UI to S3/CloudFront
